title: Grande Bleu Jeju Yacht: Honest Sunset Cruise Review
content:
Grande Bleu Jeju Yacht: The Honest Sunset Cruise Review (From Daepo Port, Not Seongsan)

Grande Bleu is Korea's only certified catamaran in Jeju, running 60-minute sunset cruises from Daepo Port in Seogwipo.

Published: today · Last updated: today By Grande Bleu Yacht Crew · Verified Local Operator

Search "Jeju yacht" and you'll get two ports: Seongsan in the east, Daepo in the south. Both are fine. But if you specifically want a sunset cruise — not a sightseeing loop — the south wins by geography. Here's why we sail from Daepo, and what you actually get for your money.

Quick Facts

  • Vessel: Twin-hull catamaran (Korea's only certified brand)
  • Duration: 60 minutes
  • Capacity: 91 guests total (Boat 550: 44 / Boat 620: 47)
  • Departure: Daepo Port, Seogwipo, Jeju (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E)
  • Price: ₩40,000–₩70,000 (adult sunset ₩70,000, child ₩40,000)
  • Includes: Free unlimited drinks, snacks, ramyeon, fishing gear
  • Crew: Licensed captain + engineer onboard
  • Track record: 100,000+ guests served since 2011

Why Daepo Port, Not Seongsan

Seongsan is the sunrise side of Jeju — the name "Seongsan Ilchulbong" literally means Sunrise Peak. Beautiful in the morning, less interesting at golden hour.

Daepo, on the south coast near Jungmun, faces west. When the sun drops toward the horizon, you don't need to reposition the boat or fight for the right side of the deck. The light comes to you.

Three practical reasons Daepo works better for sunset:

  • Orientation: West-facing route — the horizon opens up the moment you clear the harbour
  • Coastline: Volcanic cliffs, sea caves and basalt columns change colour as the light shifts
  • Sea conditions: Mt. Hallasan blocks the north wind, so the south coast stays calmer — steady enough to hold a beer on deck

If you've done the Bali sunset booze cruise or the Santorini catamaran circuit, this is the quieter cousin. Fewer boats, weirder geology, colder beer.

FAQ

What is the cheapest Jeju yacht tour? Grande Bleu's Luxury daytime tour starts at ₩60,000 per adult (₩48,000 with promo), making it one of the more affordable certified catamaran experiences in Jeju. Children ride for ₩40,000.

How long is the Jeju sunset cruise? The Grande Bleu sunset cruise runs exactly 60 minutes from Daepo Port. That's long enough to follow the entire sunset, see the basalt columns, and try the fishing without dragging on.

Is food included on the Jeju yacht? Yes — draft beer, wine, Jeju tangerine juice, water, snacks and hot ramyeon are all included with unlimited refills. No upsells or hidden bar tabs.

Where does the Jeju yacht depart from? Daepo Port in Seogwipo, on Jeju's south coast (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E). It's about 10 minutes from Jungmun resort area and 50 minutes by car from Jeju City.

Is the Jeju catamaran safe for kids and seniors? Yes. The twin-hull catamaran design has significantly less roll than a single-hull yacht, and a licensed captain and engineer are onboard every sailing. Suitable for ages 3+ and elderly guests.

What's the best time for sunset cruise in Jeju? Departure shifts with the season — earlier in winter (around 16:30–17:00), later in summer (around 19:00). The south coast is sheltered by Mt. Hallasan, so conditions are usable year-round.
